sections.paragraph:
τοίνυν (Pind., Hdt.+) a particle introducing an inference, hence, so, well (then), indeed as the second word in its clause (the usual position, so Hdt., Demosth. et al. [s. Denniston 568–77 on the position and lively, conversational aspect of τ.]; POxy 902, 10; 1252 verso, 18; Wsd 1:11; Job 8:13; 36:14; En 101:1; TestAbr A 1 p. 77, 11 [Stone p. 2]; JosAs 6:5 [1:1, 7 al. cod. A]; ApcrEzk; Jos., Ant. 2, 67; 14, 32; Ar., Just., Ath.) 1 Cor 9:26; Js 2:24 v.l.; Dg 3:2. Beginning its clause (only in later wr., e.g. Sext. Emp., Math. 8, 429; IG IV, 620, 13; POxy 940, 3; Is 3:10; 5:13; 27:4; 33:23; TestJob 41:5; Mel.) τ. ἀπόδοτε well then, give back Lk 20:25 (w. impv.); Hb 13:13 (w. hortatory subj.); 1 Cl 15:1 (w. hort. subj.)—M-M.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Denniston Denniston = JD., The Greek Particles—List 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. wr. = writers, writings

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

impv. impv. = imperative

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1009
